text: Apple unveiled the new MacBook Pro featuring the M4 Pro chip with 40% faster CPU performance. The laptop includes a redesigned thermal system and up to 24 hours of battery life.
The Federal Reserve raised interest rates by 25 basis points to combat persistent inflation. Stock markets fell sharply following the announcement as investors reassessed growth forecasts.
Manchester City defeated Real Madrid 3-1 in the UEFA Champions League semifinal. Erling Haaland scored twice in the second half to secure the dominant victory at the Etihad Stadium.
Scientists at MIT developed a new mRNA vaccine that shows 94% efficacy against multiple strains of influenza. The breakthrough could lead to a universal flu vaccine within five years.
The US Senate passed a bipartisan bill to fund border security and provide foreign aid to Ukraine and Israel. The legislation now moves to the House where it faces an uncertain future.
Netflix reported a record 22 million new subscribers in Q4, driven by hit series and its expansion into ad-supported tiers. Revenue surged 18% year-over-year to $9.8 billion.
A magnitude 7.4 earthquake struck southeastern Turkey, causing widespread destruction in three provinces. Rescue teams are searching through rubble as the death toll continues to rise.
Amazon Web Services launched a new family of AI inference chips that deliver 3x better price-performance than NVIDIA H100 GPUs for LLM workloads. The chips are available in all AWS regions.
NASA's Artemis III mission successfully landed two astronauts on the lunar south pole for the first time in history. The crew conducted 72 hours of surface operations collecting ice samples.
The Paris Olympics broke all previous viewership records with 3.7 billion people tuning in globally. France won 16 gold medals, its best performance in Olympic history.
